kept.text:
i can't believe they're really gonna do it again

the last time stripe shipped a primitive this big, it literally birthed modern saas.

shopify, substack, gumroad, all your fav indie hackers...

they exist only because stripe's payments api let them easily accept money with a few lines of code

yesterday stripe shipped the spending version.

anyone can now get an agent to spend money on their behalf.

if stripe's payments api created saas, this spending api creates autonomous commerce:

a new category of businesses that run on agents buying, booking, restocking, and paying on your behalf

to make that concrete for you, here's some cool ideas you can build now:

1. ai ad managers. you connect your meta, google, and tiktok accounts, set a monthly budget cap, and an agent runs your entire paid strategy.

2. ai procurement agents for ecommerce. they monitor supplier prices, auto-order when costs hit your threshold, send you a morning summary of what they bought and why

3. ai travel agents that actually work. they search, compare, book, and pay within your budget rules. no more toggling between 6 tabs to save $40 on a flight

4. ai bookkeeping agents. they handle the recurring operational payments your business already makes every month (contractor invoices, ad account top-ups, subscriptions, etc)

the 18-month window after a new primitive ships is historically when the category-defining companies get built

if i was building right now i'd pick one of these, find the narrowest possible version of it, and ship before it closes

generational opportunities here imo. good luck!

kept.text:
Les gars, aujourd'hui je vous partage LE tweet que j'aurais aimé trouver quand j'ai voulu faire ma LLC.

Je vais pas expliquer à quoi ça sert, juste comment j'ai procédé et les trucs importants.

Pour ouvrir une LLC et utiliser Shopify Payments US, il vous faut 2 documents : l'EIN et l'ITIN.

— L'EIN, c'est l'équivalent du numéro d'immatriculation sur un KBIS
— L'ITIN, c'est votre numéro fiscal US pour les non-résidents
1) Ouvrir la LLC

Après avoir bien comparé les prix, deux options se démarquent :
— Un prestataire Fiverr (jamais testé, risque d'arnaque)
— Bizee (la solution que j'ai prise)

Bizee fait ça depuis plus de 20 ans. Je suis passé par eux, mes amis aussi, tout est nickel. La société est ouverte en moins de 2 semaines. Ensuite, il faut patienter encore 1 à 2 semaines pour recevoir le document IRS Notice CP575G — la preuve officielle que vous avez votre EIN.
J'ai suivi cette vidéo YouTube qui explique très bien : https://t.co/M9LkWKnr5o
Lien de parrainage du mec de la vidéo (réduction pour vous, commission pour lui — sa vidéo est nickel) : https://t.co/O0yxMyFpmC

J'ai pris le service Premium à 400$ (350$ avec le lien).

Une fois la LLC ouverte et les documents reçus, vous pouvez ouvrir Stripe, Airwallex, Slash, Mercury, etc.

2) L'ITIN

Pas obligatoire pour commencer, mais d'après beaucoup de retours, il vaut mieux le commander et l'attendre avant d'ouvrir votre SP US — sans ça, les risques de contrôle sont beaucoup plus élevés.

Je suis passé par un prestataire assez connu. À savoir : l'ITIN prend entre 1 et 3 mois (parfois plus). Ce n'est pas le prestataire qui est lent, c'est le process en lui-même.

J'ai un lien d'affiliation qui fait passer le prix de 350 à 300$, vous êtes pas obligés de le prendre — c'est juste mieux pour vous, et ça m'arrange aussi mdr : https://t.co/dWGo639XUU

3) L'adresse physique

Là où j'ai galéré. J'ai essayé d'ouvrir un SP avec l'adresse mise par Bizee sur la LLC : flag, refusé direct. Pour les US, il faut une vraie adresse physique.

J'ai fini par trouver un prestataire qui facture 2k (j'ai payé) et ça a fonctionné. Mais en échangeant avec la commu, et notamment @Kaiso_ecom, il m'a mis sur ce site qui fait exactement la même chose pour 60$/mois : https://t.co/K9tfYcmXd7

J'ai pas testé, mais je suis quasi sûr que ça fonctionne nickel.

Une fois tout ça en main, vous êtes parés : LLC en règle, comptes bancaires, SP US qui fonctionne.

Voilà les amis 💪

profile_bio.description: Te ayudamos a invertir en la Bolsa de Valores.

kept.text:
Remove @Shopify from Canada's ten most valuable companies and the average age of what's left is 138 years.

To put this into perspective, 138 years ago, Alberta and Saskatchewan didn't yet exist. The high-tech infrastructure of that era was the Canadian Pacific Railway: nation-defining, audacious, and widely considered impossible before the last spike went in.

Those companies deserve respect for what they've survived. But staying alive through every technological wave in modern history does something to a culture. When your institution is older than the country itself, you start to see yourself as a steward. There are centuries of people who built what you now inherited. Protecting it feels like the right thing to do.

That instinct is understandable. But it changes everything about how risk gets evaluated, how decisions get made, what gets defined as success and what urgency means.

Canada was built by people who looked at unmapped territory and decided to go anyway. That mindset still exists, it's just been dormant.  

Let's bring it back.

"More of a steward than a builder" is the title of my latest conversation with @TheHubCanada.  

Thank you @faliceemo for the wonderful conversation.

profile_bio.description:
Founder, father, husband and former national team athlete.

All opinions are my own.

kept.text:
Why most products won’t take you from $1M to $2M on organic:

Organic only works for a specific kind of product, and most products aren’t it.

Think wearable blanket vs. regular blanket.
Think impossible non-stick pan vs. regular pan.

The second one in each pair works because it has:

- Clear before and after
- Clear stakes
- Clear visual elements

Organic scales products people can understand and want in seconds.

kept.text:
MRR (white hat) brands moving to CheckoutChamp and Phoenix is almost inevitable with everything happening with shopify payments.

I've seen dudes with $2M+ MRR overnight lose it all. 

Zero access to their customer's keys, 8-figs in profit poof like dust 💨

If you're not building backup infrastructure as a supplement brand you're bound to get cooked at some point. 

Even if you don't use it right away, it's good to be set up in case you need to switch quickly. 

If you need help my DM's are open

tweet.text:
Shopify will freeze your funds the moment you push a fresh store past €4K/day.

Don't scale one store to €10K if it's fresh. 

You're asking to get your money locked.

The move: run 2-3 stores at €3-4K each instead of one store at €10K.

Same total revenue. Way less risk.

Payment processors don't flag you. Cash flow stays clean. You can actually sustain the growth.

Once you're at €300K/month and have proper payment processing, then you can push one store harder.

But if you're still trying to hit your first €100K month? 

Multiple stores at lower volume beats one store 
getting held.

Scale smarter boss.

profile_bio.description: Scaling fashion stores behind the scenes. Focused on backend, systems & long-term growth.
